substep.title: Establish the Comparison Criteria
substep.action: If criteria were supplied in the dispatch, use them. Otherwise derive them from the decision context and the nature of the alternatives — the dimensions on which this class of choice actually differs and that the decision actually cares about. Typical dimensions for a technology choice: capability fit, performance characteristics, operational cost, ecosystem and maturity, integration effort, licensing/lock-in, failure modes. Select the criteria that matter for THIS decision; do not pad the grid with dimensions no one will weigh.

substep.title: Scope Gate
substep.action:
A matrix has to cohere on one screen of judgment. If the alternatives or criteria are so numerous that no single matrix would be legible (rough guide: beyond ~5 alternatives or ~8 criteria), emit to stdout and stop:
SCOPE_TOO_LARGE Reason: the comparison grid is too large to cohere Suggested split:

check.template-output:
Present the alternatives and the derived criteria for confirmation before researching: - The alternatives - The criteria, each with a one-line definition of what it measures - Which alternatives will need web research (flag for step 3)
[a] Adjust (add/drop/redefine criteria) [c] Continue [y] YOLO
